wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:3:{s:7:"widgets";a:6:{i:0;a:10:{s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:4:"text";s:1405:"

HOPE IN NORTH EAST CHESHIRE

Hope in North East Cheshire (Hope) encourages shared initiatives in mission and service and organises joint gatherings for worship and prayer. It also responds to requests for information, help and funding, working closely with many voluntary organisations, a number represented here, and the statutory authorities.
Incorporating Churches Together in Macclesfield and Bollington, and affiliated to Churches Together in Britain and Ireland, Hope was established in 2009 bringing together some thirty-nine Churches of many denominations in Macclesfield, Bollington and the surrounding villages. The Church Leaders meet regularly for prayer, worship, fellowship and discussion, while small groups of Leaders and lay people plan Hope’s priorities, review progress and relationships, and manage its finances.
Hope involves lots of volunteers, many from the local Churches, in a wide range of initiatives which any one Church would find difficult to sustain. It encourages like-minded people to come together to respond to their calling and to share their passions to make things happen for the benefit of the community.
Proclaiming the Gospel through Social Action in Macclesfield and Bollington and the surrounding villages.

item.title: Church locations
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded: [siteorigin_widget class="WP_Widget_Custom_HTML"][/siteorigin_widget]

item.title: Objectives
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
Brings together some thirty-nine Churches in Macclesfield and Bollington and the surrounding villages who work together in mission in their communities. The objectives of Hope in North East Cheshire represent a visible sign of the Churches' commitment to each other by:
  • providing a Church Leaders’ Forum for consultation and policy-making
  • encouraging shared worship and prayer, learning, service and evangelism
  • promoting initiatives in mission and service
  • encouraging local groupings of Churches
  • strengthening sustainable working relationships and reinforcing long-term partnerships
Between them, other Christian organisations, their local communities and the providers of educational, fire, health, housing, policing and social services. Hope in North East Cheshire is affiliated to Churches Together in Britain and Ireland.

item.title: Street Angels
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
‘People deserve care and love in their time of need’

We work together with the management of Macclesfield Town Centre and the Police to help people, whoever they are and whatever their situation, by listening, caring, offering practical help or referring them to specialist agencies.

As volunteers from the local churches we walk around the town on Saturday nights (from 10.30pm - 3.00am):

  • providing a Christian presence
  • giving free bottles of water
  • helping out with free flip-flops for those who need them
  • lending a listening ear to those whose evening has not turned out so well
  • giving simple first aid
  • inviting those who need a warm drink back to the United Reformed Church, our centre
  • referring those who are in dire need to the emergency services

 

I would like to consider helping with Street Angels Macclesfield on a Saturday evening:

  • Please contact your church leader or administrator
  • email streetangelsmacclesfield@gmail.com
  • or call Pip Mosscrop Street Angels coordinator on 07715 495701
[siteorigin_widget class="SiteOrigin_Widget_Image_Widget"][/siteorigin_widget]
[siteorigin_widget class="SiteOrigin_Widget_Image_Widget"][/siteorigin_widget]

‘People deserve care and love in their time of need’

We work together with the management of Macclesfield Town Centre and the Police to help people, whoever they are and whatever their situation, by listening, caring, offering practical help or referring them to specialist agencies.

As volunteers from the local churches we walk around the town on Saturday nights (from 10.30pm - 3.00am):

  • providing a Christian presence
  • giving free bottles of water
  • helping out with free flip-flops for those who need them
  • lending a listening ear to those whose evening has not turned out so well
  • giving simple first aid
  • inviting those who need a warm drink back to the United Reformed Church, our centre

  • Please contact your church leader or administrator
  • email streetangelsmacclesfield@gmail.com
  • or call Pip Mosscrop Street Angels coordinator on 07715 495701

‘People deserve care and love in their time of need’

We work together with the management of Macclesfield Town Centre and the Police to help people, whoever they are and whatever their situation, by listening, caring, offering practical help or referring them to specialist agencies.

As volunteers from the local churches we walk around the town on Saturday nights (from 10.30pm - 3.00am):

  • providing a Christian presence
  • giving free bottles of water
  • helping out with free flip-flops for those who need them
  • lending a listening ear to those whose evening has not turned out so well
  • giving simple first aid
  • inviting those who need a warm drink back to the United Reformed Church, our centre
  • referring those who are in dire need to the emergency services

 

I would like to consider helping with Street Angels Macclesfield on a Saturday evening:

  • Please contact your church leader or administrator
  • email streetangelsmacclesfield@gmail.com
  • or call Pip Mosscrop Street Angels coordinator on 07715 495701

-4456-9d28-364f3b3b3650";s:5:"style";a:5:{s:27:"background_image_attachment";b:0;s:18:"background_display";s:4:"tile";s:21:"background_image_size";s:4:"full";s:24:"background_image_opacity";s:3:"100";s:16:"border_thickness";s:3:"1px";}}s:10:"link_title";s:0:"";s:10:"new_window";s:0:"";s:10:"full_width";s:0:"";s:22:"so_sidebar_emulator_id";s:17:"sow-image-6110002";s:11:"option_name";s:16:"widget_sow-image";}}s:5:"grids";a:1:{i:0;a:2:{s:5:"cells";i:2;s:5:"style";a:3:{s:11:"full_height";s:0:"";s:23:"background_image_repeat";s:0:"";s:9:"no_margin";s:0:"";}}}s:10:"grid_cells";a:2:{i:0;a:4:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:5:"index";i:0;s:6:"weight";d:0.61803399209206;s:5:"style";a:0:{}}i:1;a:4:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:5:"index";i:1;s:6:"weight";d:0.38196600790794;s:5:"style";a:0:{}}}}

item.title: Open The Book
item.dc:creator: john

item.title: Cheshire Streetwise Furniture Project
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded: With the support of Churches and volunteers in Macclesfield, Cheshire Streetwise is combating poverty through the provision of furniture and other household items to those in need in the areas of Macclesfield and Congleton.   The furniture project contributes items to the Emergency Assistance Scheme operated by Cheshire East and also provides furniture to individuals and families through referrals from local agencies and churches.   Volunteers currently collect and deliver furniture two to three days a week and run a shop at Gilchrist Avenue, Macclesfield for four days a week (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday 10.00-4.00, Saturday 10.00-12.00). Visitors are always welcome for a cup of tea and a chat.   Cheshire Streetwise is committed to providing education, support and counselling to a wide range of children and adults with a variety of needs and also provides tuition, a skills cafe, counselling, foodbank and signposting service.   To arrange collections, referrals or for general enquiries call 07469 176811     Like: Facebook at www.facebook.com/CheshireFurnitureReuse Follow: CheshreReuse @CFRFCoordinator Visit: http://www.cfrf.org.uk/

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:3:{s:7:"widgets";a:2:{i:0;a:6:{s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:4:"text";s:1886:"
With the support of Churches and volunteers in Macclesfield, Cheshire Streetwise is combating poverty through the provision of furniture and other household items to those in need in the areas of Macclesfield and Congleton.
 
The furniture project contributes items to the Emergency Assistance Scheme operated by Cheshire East and also provides furniture to individuals and families through referrals from local agencies and churches.
 
Volunteers currently collect and deliver furniture two to three days a week and run a shop at Gilchrist Avenue, Macclesfield for four days a week (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday 10.00-4.00, Saturday 10.00-12.00). Visitors are always welcome for a cup of tea and a chat.
 
Cheshire Streetwise is committed to providing education, support and counselling to a wide range of children and adults with a variety of needs and also provides tuition, a skills cafe, counselling, foodbank and signposting service.
 
To arrange collections, referrals or for general enquiries call 07469 176811
 
 
Like: Facebook at www.facebook.com/CheshireFurnitureReuse
Follow: CheshreReuse @CFRFCoordinator
Visit: http://www.cfrf.org.uk/
";s:20:"text_selected_editor";s:7:"tinymce";s:5:"autop";b:1;s:12:"_sow_form_id";s:13:"56d05b6e93fba";s:11:"panels_info";a:6:{s:5:"class";s:31:"SiteOrigin_Widget_Editor_Widget";s:3:"raw";b:0;s:4:"grid";i:0;s:4:"cell";i:0;s:2:"id";i:0;s:5:"style";a:1:{s:18:"background_display";s:4:"tile";}}}i:1;a:13:{s:5:"image";i:200;s:14:"image_fallback";s:0:"";s:4:"size";s:4:"full";s:5:"align";s:7:"default";s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:14:"title_position";s:6:"hidden";s:3:"alt";s:0:"";s:3:"url";s:0:"";s:5:"bound";b:1;s:12:"_sow_form_id";s:13:"579f88a46200b";s:11:"panels_info";a:5:{s:5:"class";s:30:"SiteOrigin_Widget_Image_Widget";s:4:"grid";i:0;s:4:"cell";i:1;s:2:"id";i:1;s:5:"style";a:2:{s:27:"background_image_attachment";b:0;s:18:"background_display";s:4:"tile";}}s:10:"new_window";b:0;s:10:"full_width";b:0;}}s:5:"grids";a:1:{i:0;a:2:{s:5:"cells";i:2;s:5:"style";a:2:{s:23:"background_image_repeat";s:0:"";s:9:"no_margin";s:0:"";}}}s:10:"grid_cells";a:2:{i:0;a:2:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:6:"weight";d:0.61803399209206;}i:1;a:2:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:6:"weight";d:0.38196600790794;}}}

a:3:{s:7:"widgets";a:2:{i:0;a:6:{s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:4:"text";s:1886:"
With the support of Churches and volunteers in Macclesfield, Cheshire Streetwise is combating poverty through the provision of furniture and other household items to those in need in the areas of Macclesfield and Congleton.
 
The furniture project contributes items to the Emergency Assistance Scheme operated by Cheshire East and also provides furniture to individuals and families through referrals from local agencies and churches.
 
Volunteers currently collect and deliver furniture two to three days a week and run a shop at Gilchrist Avenue, Macclesfield for four days a week (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday 10.00-4.00, Saturday 10.00-12.00). Visitors are always welcome for a cup of tea and a chat.
 
Cheshire Streetwise is committed to providing education, support and counselling to a wide range of children and adults with a variety of needs and also provides tuition, a skills cafe, counselling, foodbank and signposting service.
 
To arrange collections, referrals or for general enquiries call 07469 176811
 
 
Like: Facebook at www.facebook.com/CheshireFurnitureReuse
Follow: CheshreReuse @CFRFCoordinator
Visit: http://www.cfrf.org.uk/
";s:20:"text_selected_editor";s:7:"tinymce";s:5:"autop";b:1;s:12:"_sow_form_id";s:13:"56d05b6e93fba";s:11:"panels_info";a:6:{s:5:"class";s:31:"SiteOrigin_Widget_Editor_Widget";s:3:"raw";b:0;s:4:"grid";i:0;s:4:"cell";i:0;s:2:"id";i:0;s:5:"style";a:1:{s:18:"background_display";s:4:"tile";}}}i:1;a:13:{s:5:"image";i:200;s:14:"image_fallback";s:0:"";s:4:"size";s:4:"full";s:5:"align";s:6:"center";s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:14:"title_position";s:6:"hidden";s:3:"alt";s:0:"";s:3:"url";s:0:"";s:5:"bound";b:1;s:12:"_sow_form_id";s:13:"579f88a46200b";s:11:"panels_info";a:5:{s:5:"class";s:30:"SiteOrigin_Widget_Image_Widget";s:4:"grid";i:0;s:4:"cell";i:1;s:2:"id";i:1;s:5:"style";a:2:{s:27:"background_image_attachment";b:0;s:18:"background_display";s:4:"tile";}}s:10:"new_window";b:0;s:10:"full_width";b:0;}}s:5:"grids";a:1:{i:0;a:2:{s:5:"cells";i:2;s:5:"style";a:2:{s:23:"background_image_repeat";s:0:"";s:9:"no_margin";s:0:"";}}}s:10:"grid_cells";a:2:{i:0;a:2:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:6:"weight";d:0.61803399209206;}i:1;a:2:{s:4:"grid";i:0;s:6:"weight";d:0.38196600790794;}}}

item.title: Just Drop In
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
Background and Aims
Just Drop-In is the Millennium Project of Churches Together in Macclesfield, with the support of 14 churches. It provides a 'one-stop shop' for 12-25 year of age (and their family) in and around Macclesfield where they can access information and get specialist advice on a whole range of issues, in a welcoming and informal setting in the town centre. It opened in November 2001 at 17a Chestergate, leased for 5 years. We are now in brand new premises with the Youth Service at 33 Great King Street, Macclesfield, SK11 6PN.

Operation
JDI employs a full-time Co-ordinator, supported by a part-time volunteer co-ordinator/administrator and approximately 35 volunteers working 3-hour shifts. It is open every day except Sunday. Although run by the churches, it is in partnership with Cheshire Youth Services, Macclesfield Accommodation Care and Concern (the Glegg Street Hostel) and other agencies who are available at the centre on specified days of the week. Two youth workers from Cheshire Youth Service are generally at JDI.

Young people can be helped with accommodation, employment, drug and alcohol abuse, bullying, relationships, benefits, mental health and any other areas in which they need information and advice. Six computers are available for access to information on the internet and for use for homework, CV’s, projects, presentations, job applications, etc. The Cyber Café has proved very popular. The number of weekly visits has increased from an initial 20 to a current 120 or more.

The co-ordinator identified the lack of a professional counselling service for young people in the area. After intensive consultation with agencies, extra funding was sought and obtained. Lisa Gaffney, an experienced BACP accredited counsellor was appointed part time and started the service in July 2004. She is kept very busy.

Just Drop-In is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 1400-1700; Tuesday and Thursday 1200-1800; Saturday 1000-1200.

Accreditation
Just Drop-In has obtained the Legal Services Commission Quality Kitemark and Validation by the Cheshire Council for Voluntary Youth Service

Finance
Just Drop-In opened with a Community Fund Grant which covered start-up and running costs for three years. This expired in March 2004. Since then we have been using our reserve fund built up by local fund raising, donations and grants.

The Trustees are now looking to secure the longer term future of Just Drop-In, which has now established itself as a focal point for the youth of the town. In three years it can be said to have helped many young lives, and in many cases has made a vital difference.

We are always looking for fund-raising initiatives and support.

Visit the Just Drop-In web site

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:

Background and Aims
Just Drop-In is the Millennium Project of Churches Together in Macclesfield, with the support of 14 churches. It provides a 'one-stop shop' for 12-25 year of age (and their family) in and around Macclesfield where they can access information and get specialist advice on a whole range of issues, in a welcoming and informal setting in the town centre. It opened in November 2001 at 17a Chestergate, leased for 5 years. We are now in brand new premises with the Youth Service at 33 Great King Street, Macclesfield, SK11 6PN.

Operation
JDI employs a full-time Co-ordinator, supported by a part-time volunteer co-ordinator/administrator and approximately 35 volunteers working 3-hour shifts. It is open every day except Sunday. Although run by the churches, it is in partnership with Cheshire Youth Services, Macclesfield Accommodation Care and Concern (the Glegg Street Hostel) and other agencies who are available at the centre on specified days of the week. Two youth workers from Cheshire Youth Service are generally at JDI.

Young people can be helped with accommodation, employment, drug and alcohol abuse, bullying, relationships, benefits, mental health and any other areas in which they need information and advice. Six computers are available for access to information on the internet and for use for homework, CV’s, projects, presentations, job applications, etc. The Cyber Café has proved very popular. The number of weekly visits has increased from an initial 20 to a current 120 or more.

The co-ordinator identified the lack of a professional counselling service for young people in the area. After intensive consultation with agencies, extra funding was sought and obtained. Lisa Gaffney, an experienced BACP accredited counsellor was appointed part time and started the service in July 2004. She is kept very busy.

Just Drop-In is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 1400-1700; Tuesday and Thursday 1200-1800; Saturday 1000-1200.

Accreditation
Just Drop-In has obtained the Legal Services Commission Quality Kitemark and Validation by the Cheshire Council for Voluntary Youth Service

Finance
Just Drop-In opened with a Community Fund Grant which covered start-up and running costs for three years. This expired in March 2004. Since then we have been using our reserve fund built up by local fund raising, donations and grants.

The Trustees are now looking to secure the longer term future of Just Drop-In, which has now established itself as a focal point for the youth of the town. In three years it can be said to have helped many young lives, and in many cases has made a vital difference.

We are always looking for fund-raising initiatives and support.

Visit the Just Drop-In web site

Background and Aims
Just Drop-In is the Millennium Project of Churches Together in Macclesfield, with the support of 14 churches. It provides a 'one-stop shop' for 12-25 year of age (and their family) in and around Macclesfield where they can access information and get specialist advice on a whole range of issues, in a welcoming and informal setting in the town centre. It opened in November 2001 at 17a Chestergate, leased for 5 years. We are now in brand new premises with the Youth Service at 33 Great King Street, Macclesfield, SK11 6PN.

Operation
JDI employs a full-time Co-ordinator, supported by a part-time volunteer co-ordinator/administrator and approximately 35 volunteers working 3-hour shifts. It is open every day except Sunday. Although run by the churches, it is in partnership with Cheshire Youth Services, Macclesfield Accommodation Care and Concern (the Glegg Street Hostel) and other agencies who are available at the centre on specified days of the week. Two youth workers from Cheshire Youth Service are generally at JDI.

Young people can be helped with accommodation, employment, drug and alcohol abuse, bullying, relationships, benefits, mental health and any other areas in which they need information and advice. Six computers are available for access to information on the internet and for use for homework, CV’s, projects, presentations, job applications, etc. The Cyber Café has proved very popular. The number of weekly visits has increased from an initial 20 to a current 120 or more.

The co-ordinator identified the lack of a professional counselling service for young people in the area. After intensive consultation with agencies, extra funding was sought and obtained. Lisa Gaffney, an experienced BACP accredited counsellor was appointed part time and started the service in July 2004. She is kept very busy.

Just Drop-In is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 1400-1700; Tuesday and Thursday 1200-1800; Saturday 1000-1200.

Accreditation
Just Drop-In has obtained the Legal Services Commission Quality Kitemark and Validation by the Cheshire Council for Voluntary Youth Service

Finance
Just Drop-In opened with a Community Fund Grant which covered start-up and running costs for three years. This expired in March 2004. Since then we have been using our reserve fund built up by local fund raising, donations and grants.

The Trustees are now looking to secure the longer term future of Just Drop-In, which has now established itself as a focal point for the youth of the town. In three years it can be said to have helped many young lives, and in many cases has made a vital difference.

We are always looking for fund-raising initiatives and support.

Visit the Just Drop-In web site

item.title: The Hope Centre
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
The HOPE Centre welcome everyone to it's coffee shop and Christian bookshop, selling books, cards, and gifts. Based at Park Green, Macclesfield, it provides a beacon of hope to the people of Macclesfield.

We stock a range of Christian resources (available and to order) including Bibles in various translations, Bible reading notes, Junior church, church resources, holiday clubs, messy church, study guides for house groups. We have Bible commentaries, concordances, study guides, Childrens activity books, pocket money gifts and novelty items, CDs and music resources.

A large selection of cards is available for all occasions: Gifts including first communion, confirmation, baptism, jewellry, games, seasonal presents, stationery, mass cards and rosaries. 

We will also order any book or Bible from our suppliers.

Visit The Hope Centre website.

We stock a range of Christian resources (available and to order) including Bibles in various translations, Bible reading notes, Junior church, church resources, holiday clubs, messy church, study guides for house groups. We have Bible commentaries, concordances, study guides, Childrens activity books, pocket money gifts and novelty items, CDs and music resources.

A large selection of cards is available for all occasions: Gifts including first communion, confirmation, baptism, jewellry, games, seasonal presents, stationery, mass cards and rosaries. 

We will also order any book or Bible from our suppliers.

Visit The Hope Centre website.

We stock a range of Christian resources (available and to order) including Bibles in various translations, Bible reading notes, Junior church, church resources, holiday clubs, messy church, study guides for house groups. We have Bible commentaries, concordances, study guides, Childrens activity books, pocket money gifts and novelty items, CDs and music resources.

item.title: Foodbank
item.dc:creator: john

item.title: CRE8
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
Cre8 is an independent charity based on the Moss Rose Estate in Macclesfield. We provide projects and activities for young people and their families. Our work is aimed towards empowering young people and adults in the local area to make change happen for themselves and also for their local neighbourhood.

The Cre8 community is made up of a wide range of people from different backgrounds, who work or volunteer from full time to just a couple of hours over the holidays. From the wounderful, generous and passionate volunteers who are the backbone of everything we do, to the slightly crazy but dedicated and very patient staff team - everyone who has any involvement with Cre8 is a part of the team.

Visit the CRE8 website.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
a:3:{s:7:"widgets";a:2:{i:0;a:6:{s:5:"title";s:0:"";s:4:"text";s:810:"

Cre8 is an independent charity based on the Moss Rose Estate in Macclesfield. We provide projects and activities for young people and their families. Our work is aimed towards empowering young people and adults in the local area to make change happen for themselves and also for their local neighbourhood.

The Cre8 community is made up of a wide range of people from different backgrounds, who work or volunteer from full time to just a couple of hours over the holidays. From the wounderful, generous and passionate volunteers who are the backbone of everything we do, to the slightly crazy but dedicated and very patient staff team - everyone who has any involvement with Cre8 is a part of the team.

Visit the CRE8 website.

Cre8 is an independent charity based on the Moss Rose Estate in Macclesfield. We provide projects and activities for young people and their families. Our work is aimed towards empowering young people and adults in the local area to make change happen for themselves and also for their local neighbourhood.

The Cre8 community is made up of a wide range of people from different backgrounds, who work or volunteer from full time to just a couple of hours over the holidays. From the wounderful, generous and passionate volunteers who are the backbone of everything we do, to the slightly crazy but dedicated and very patient staff team - everyone who has any involvement with Cre8 is a part of the team.

Visit the CRE8 website.

item.title: What's Going On? Social Action Marketplace
item.dc:creator: terry.gibson
item.content:encoded: One of Macclesfield’s best kept secrets is the amount of social action being provided by small local organisations, and the huge amount of volunteering to provide help where it’s needed. You can access the booklet about the organisations, a video of their work, and contact details below: What's going on? Information booklet What's going on? Organisation details This video features short introductions to 11 organisations who are participating in the 'What's going on?' event https://youtu.be/jpE-8GGg6v8

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe width="720" height="405" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/jpE-8GGg6v8?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" gesture="media" allow="encrypted-media" allowfullscreen></iframe>

<iframe title="whats going on video version 2" width="500" height="281" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/jpE-8GGg6v8?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: What's going on? - the video
item.dc:creator: terry.gibson
item.content:encoded:
This film gives a taste of the work of 11 local organisations featured in the 'What's going on? event.
https://youtu.be/jpE-8GGg6v8

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe title="whats going on video version 2" width="500" height="281" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/jpE-8GGg6v8?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: 'What's going on' Video
item.dc:creator: terry.gibson
item.content:encoded:
This video features short introductions to 11 organisations who are participating in the 'What's going on?' event
https://youtu.be/jpE-8GGg6v8

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe width="720" height="405" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/jpE-8GGg6v8?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" gesture="media" allow="encrypted-media" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: 'What's Going On?' contact info
item.dc:creator: terry.gibson
item.content:encoded:
Here are contact emails for the organisations featured in the 'What's going on' event
Organisation Email
Cre8 Katy.wardle@cre8macclesfield.org
WHAM david.wightman1@ntlworld.com
Street Angels pipmosscrop2@yahoo.co.uk
Treehouse louise.wiliamson6@aol.co.uk

  • Facebook: www.facebook.com/cre8macclesfield

item.title: Upcoming events
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
<!--<iframe src="https://calendar.google.com/calendar/embed?showTitle=0&height=700&wkst=2&bgcolor=%23FFFFFF&src=epdnudblnpqlfmb0flq6420dpo%40group.calendar.google.com&color=%23182C57&ctz=Europe%2FLondon" style="border-width:0" width="950" height="700" frameborder="0" scrolling="no"></iframe>-->

item.title: Events
item.dc:creator: john

item.title: Strategic Priorities
item.dc:creator: john
item.content:encoded:
Our strategic priorities for the next 3 years

  1. Continue to provide an environment that encourages church leaders to meet together and to selflessly support and help each other.
  2. Grow a stronger relationship with leaders and churches who currently are not active within Hope.
  3. Build stronger links between the churches and communities that we serve, including through even greater sharing of resources, ideas and best practice.
  4. More clearly communicate our purpose, plans and stories to churches and local communities including through greater use of social media.
  5. Provide opportunities for existing church members to grow in faith through service and shared discipleship.
  6. Create and deliver a credible, resourceable plan to reach out with the gospel message to people in the community (e.g. hold at least 1 town-wide outreach event pa and grow the Treacle Market prayer-walking initiative).
  7. Re-draw our plan for social action, identify new activities and adjusting existing activities... to best meet community needs, especially for the disadvantaged and marginalised.
  8. Keep things simple, low in formality, true and Spirit-led.

item.title: Churches
item.dc:creator: john
